{
  "analysis": "pip not found, need to use pip3. Let me try that approach and also use a different method to check the VM state.",
  "plan": "Install vncdotool with pip3 and use it to capture the display. Also try a direct framebuffer approach.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "pip3 install vncdotool 2>&1 | tail -5
",
      "duration": 30.0
    },
    {
      "keystrokes": "python3 -c "
import socket, struct, time

sock = socket.socket(socket.AF_INET, socket.SOCK_STREAM)
sock.connect(('localhost', 5901))

# Read protocol version
version = sock.recv(12)
print(f'VNC Version: {version}')

# Send supported version
sock.send(b'RFB 003.008\n')

# Read security types
sec_types = sock.recv(256)
print(f'Security types: {list(sec_types)}')

# Choose None (1)
sock.send(bytes([1]))

# Security result
result = sock.recv(4)
print(f'Security result: {struct.unpack(">I", result)}')

# Send shared flag
sock.send(bytes([1]))

# Server init
init = sock.recv(256)
width = struct.unpack('>H', init[0:2])[0]
height = struct.unpack('>H', init[2:4])[0]
print(f'Resolution: {width}x{height}')
print(f'Init data length: {len(init)}')

# Name
name_len = struct.unpack('>I', init[20:24])[0]
name = init[24:24+name_len]
print(f'Name: {name}')

sock.close()
" 2>&1
",
      "duration": 5.0
    }
  ],
  "task_complete": false
}
